channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Takeaways from Blinken's latest Mideast trip, possibly his last to the region as the top US diplomat
item.description: AQABA, Jordan (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken has been on perhaps his last Middle East diplomatic mission as America’s top diplomat this week with the aim of preventing Syria from spiraling out of control after the sudden ouster of President Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump will attend the Army-Navy game alongside key allies, nominees and Daniel Penny
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump will huddle with allies and a Republican cause célèbre at Saturday's Army-Navy football game, a chance to take in one of most stories rivalries in college sports while spotlighting his emerging national security team.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Mother of suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing spoke with FBI the night before her son's arrest: report
item.description: The FBI questioned the mother of Luigi Mangione the night before her son's arrest in Pennsylvania, and she said he bore a resemblance to the wanted suspect.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Drones, planes or UFOs? Americans abuzz over mysterious New Jersey sightings
item.description: CHATHAM, N.J. (AP) — That buzzing coming out of New Jersey? It's unclear if it's drones or something else, but for sure the nighttime sightings are producing tons of talk, a raft of conspiracy theories and craned necks looking skyward.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Detroit area's many Syrians are celebrating Assad's overthrow and planning long-delayed visits
item.description: DEARBORN, Mich. (AP) — Nizam Abazid is gleefully planning his first trip in decades to Syria, where he grew up. Rama Alhoussaini was only 6 years old when her family moved to the U.S., but she's excited about the prospect of introducing her three kids to relatives they've never met in person.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS and MIKE HOUSEHOLDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says GOP will push to eliminate daylight saving time
item.description: President-elect Trump on Friday said Republicans would push to eliminate daylight saving time, calling it "inconvenient" and "costly.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Supreme Court will take up a challenge related to California's tough vehicle emissions standards
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Friday said it would take up a business-backed appeal that could make it easier to challenge federal regulations, acting in a dispute related to California's nation-leading standards for vehicle emissions.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas AG sues New York doctor for providing abortion pills across state lines
item.description: Both surgical and medication abortions are banned in Texas, and Friday’s lawsuit is the first legal test to see what happens when state abortion laws conflict with one another.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, KRON4

item.title: Biden's commutation in 'kids for cash' scandal angers some Pennsylvania families
item.description: A judge who helped orchestrate one of the worst judicial scandals in U.S.history — a scheme to send children to for-profit jails in exchange for kickbacks — was among the 1,500 people whose sentences were commuted by President Joe Biden this week.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL RUBINKAM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: San Francisco police investigator recognized Mangione from missing person poster: source
item.description: A San Francisco police investigator recognized Luigi Mangione from a wanted bulletin poster days before his arrest, a source tells Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: RFK Jr.'s daughter-in-law met with Ratcliffe about CIA job: Washington Post
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s daughter-in-law took a private meeting with John Ratcliffe, President-elect Trump’s pick to run the CIA, in an apparent bid for a high-ranking position within the agency, The Washington Post reported.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, Fox 5

item.title: WarnerBros. Discovery announces major corporate restructure
item.description: Media conglomerate WarnerBros. Discovery (WBD) announced a major restructuring of its business on Thursday, splitting its linear and streaming arms into two separate companies as it eyes potential mergers and acquisitions in the coming year.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Secretly recorded videos are backbone of corruption trial for longest-serving legislative leader
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Hours of secretly recorded videos and phone calls have offered a rare glimpse into how the longest-serving legislative leader in American history operated behind closed doors.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 men go free after their convictions were overturned in a case involving a discredited detective
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Kan. (AP) — Two men were freed Wednesday after their convictions were overturned in a 2009 double homicide whose investigation was overseen by a discredited white Kansas City, Kansas, police detective.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Homeland Security shares new details of mysterious drone flights over New Jersey
item.description: The large mysterious drones reported flying over parts of New Jersey in recent weeks appear to avoid detection by traditional methods such as helicopter and radio, according to a state lawmaker briefed Wednesday by the Department of Homeland Security.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bill Belichick, University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill finalize coaching deal: reports
item.description: Bill Belichick has reportedly been tapped as the next head coach for the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill.
item.dc:creator: Brayden Stamps, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FIFA confirms Saudi Arabia as 2034 World Cup host despite human rights concerns
item.description: ZURICH (AP) — Saudi Arabia was officially confirmed Wednesday by FIFA as host of the 2034 World Cup in men’s soccer, giving the oil-rich kingdom its biggest prize yet for massive spending on global sports driven by Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM DUNBAR, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Wisconsin kayaker who faked his death and fled the country is charged after returning to US
item.description: GREEN LAKE, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin man who faked his own drowning and left his wife and three children for Eastern Europe willingly returned to the U.S. after four months and was charged Wednesday with obstructing an intense lake search for his body.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing could return to New York on a governor's warrant
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The suspect in the killing of the UnitedHealthcare CEO plans to fight extradition to New York to face murder charges, but officials hope to get him back with what’s called a governor’s warrant. The process could happen quickly or take more than a month. New York Gov.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Will TikTok be banned in January? Here's where things stand as deadline nears
item.description: You may not be able to doomscroll on TikTok much longer unless the social media company finds a way to stop a new law from going into effect.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KTLA 5

item.title: Christopher Wray to resign as FBI director ahead of Trump takeover
item.description: FBI Director Christopher Wray announced Wednesday he plans to resign from the agency, leaving his post ahead of President-Trump's apparent plans to fire him.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, ABC 4

item.title: The Amazing Kreskin, renowned mentalist and frequent late-night guest, dies at 89
item.description: The Amazing Kreskin, an entertainer and mentalist who rose to fame amid frequent appearances on TV talk shows like “The Tonight Show” and “The Mike Douglas Show,” has died, his family confirmed on social media. He was 89 years old.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Albertsons gives up on Kroger merger, sues the grocery chain
item.description: Kroger and Albertsons in 2022 proposed what would be the largest grocery store merger in U.S. history. The companies said a merger would help them better compete with big retailers like Walmart, Costco and Amazon.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Dick Van Dyke, Cher among those forced to flee Malibu homes in wake of wildfire
item.description: Officials said the fire, which grew significantly overnight, was only 7% contained as of Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, Fox 5

item.title: Luigi Mangione's fingerprints match water bottle found near shooting: sources
item.description: Fingerprints taken from Luigi Mangione in Pennsylvania matched those on a water bottle found near the scene of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson's fatal shooting, sources said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, Fox 5

item.title: Your daily cup of coffee could get more expensive because of climate change
item.description: Coffee lovers and café dwellers are likely to see the price of a cup of joe increase soon, experts warn.
item.dc:creator: Maya Eaglin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sanders says this will likely be his last term
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.), one of the Senate’s most prominent progressives, says he likely won’t run again for the Senate after his fourth term expires in 2030.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WGN 9

item.title: Luigi Mangione was reported missing 2 weeks before CEO slain: police
item.description: The parents of Luigi Mangione, the man suspected of shooting and killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, told law enforcement that they hadn't heard from their son in months.
item.dc:creator: Video credit: Nicole Johnson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rocky Colavito, 9-time All-Star outfielder and one of Cleveland's most popular athletes, dies at 91
item.description: Rocky Colavito, a wildly popular outfielder who was involved in one of the most debated trades in Cleveland sports history, has died. He was 91.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: Only about 2 in 10 Americans approve of Biden's pardon of his son Hunter, an AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Only about 2 in 10 Americans approve of President Joe Biden’s decision to pardon his son Hunter after earlier promising he would do no such thing, according to a poll from The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: After acquittal in subway chokehold trial, Daniel Penny says he was vulnerable in the encounter
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — After being acquitted of homicide, the military veteran who choked a volatile, mentally ill man on a New York subway told an interviewer he put himself in a “very vulnerable position” but felt compelled to act.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and SANYA MANSOOR, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Oregon federal judge temporarily blocks proposed Kroger-Albertsons merger
item.description: Oregon was one of several states to join the Federal Trade Commission’s lawsuit that aimed to block the merger.
item.dc:creator: Jashayla Pettigrew, Fox 5

item.title: FBI says 2 Homeland Security agents in Utah sold illegal drugs for profit through informant
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Two Department of Homeland Security agents in Utah sold drugs that were previously seized as evidence through an informant in an illegal scheme that brought in up to $300,000, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: When fire threatened a California university, the school says it knew what to do
item.description: MALIBU, Calif. (AP) — The wildfire alert came in the middle of the night as some college students in Southern California were cramming for final exams and others were woken up in their dorms.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN and ERIC THAYER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mangione family in Maryland: What we know
item.description: Although many people outside of Maryland came to know the "Mangione" name only after the arrest of Luigi Mangione, 26, who is accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, the family has a number of prominent ties to the state where he grew up.
item.dc:creator: Brian Farrell, Fox 5

item.title: Las Vegas judge dismisses infamous 'Black Widow' murder case after 27 years
item.description: The judge dismissed murder charges against Margaret Rudin, the woman dubbed "The Black Widow" who a jury convicted of killing her husband more than two decades ago.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, Fox 5
